channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Melbourne teenager pleads guilty to fatal crash and violent assault causing lifelong injury
item.description: A Melbourne teenager has pleaded guilty to a series of serious offences over a three-month period that resulted in the death of a 16-year-old boy and left another man with permanent disabilities. The ...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Diplomatic Relations with Algeria and Zambia
item.description: This week, Tanzania demonstrated strong diplomatic engagement in Africa as two newly appointed ambassadors officially started their duties in Algeria and Zambia. Ambassador Mobhare Matinyi presented h...

item.title: Government Seeks Investors for Major Redevelopment Projects in Msasani and Sinza Areas of Dar es Salaam
item.description: The Tanzanian government has began efforts to find suitable investors for big redevelopment works in Msasani and Sinza neighbourhoods within Dar es Salaam. This forms part of larger plans to modernise...

item.title: Ajit Doval Engages Russian Cosmonauts on Space Experience and Human Perception
item.description: During his official visit to Moscow, National Security Advisor Ajit Doval engaged with Russian cosmonauts aboard the International Space Station, reflecting the deepening cooperation between India and...

item.title: Analyst says Trump limits public college appearances amid concerns over audience reactions
item.description: According to an analysis cited in MS NOW, former President Donald Trump is increasingly avoiding public appearances at civilian college campuses, a shift described as a significant departure from the ...

item.title: Suki Waterhouse credits ‘Daisy Jones And The Six’ and personal life for renewed music career focus
item.description: Suki Waterhouse has spoken about how her role in the television series ‘Daisy Jones And The Six’ played a significant part in encouraging her to take her music career more seriously. In the show, she ...

item.title: Bret Michaels Withdraws from Freedom 250 Concert Citing Safety Concerns
item.description: Bret Michaels, the lead singer of Poison, has announced he will no longer perform at the 'Freedom 250' festival, making him the fifth of nine initially announced artists to withdraw. Michaels cited vi...

item.title: Experiencing Come From Away in Gander: a unique theatre and community journey
item.description: This article explores the deeply personal and emotional experience of seeing the musical Come From Away performed in Gander, Newfoundland and Labrador, the town where the true events behind the show t...

item.title: Inquest opens into deaths of three sisters in the sea off Brighton
item.description: An inquest has opened into the deaths of three sisters who died in the sea off Brighton beach in May. Jane Adetoro, 36, Christina Walters, 32, and Rebecca Walters, 31, all from Greenford, London, were...

item.title: Woman and domestic worker sentenced to life for murdering Pretoria man over insurance payout
item.description: In a high-profile case in Pretoria, a 42-year-old woman, Ntabiseng Tlali, and her 39-year-old domestic worker, Lineo Ralitsa, have both been sentenced to life imprisonment for the premeditated murder ...

item.title: 10-year-old Olongapo gymnast wins two gold medals in rhythmic gymnastics at Palarong Pambansa debut
item.description: A 10-year-old gymnast from Olongapo City, Gabrielle Louie Mazon of Central Luzon, made an impressive debut at the 2026 Palarong Pambansa in Agusan del Sur by winning two gold medals in rhythmic gymnas...

item.title: New 98-million-year-old wasp species from Burmese amber named after F1 driver Oscar Piastri
item.description: A newly identified species of wasp dating back to the age of the dinosaurs has been discovered preserved in Burmese amber and named in honour of Formula One driver Oscar Piastri. The fossil, estimated...

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel comments on controversy and cancellations surrounding Trump's Freedom 250 festival
item.description: Late-night host Jimmy Kimmel addressed growing controversy surrounding the Freedom 250 festival, a large-scale event planned to celebrate the 250th anniversary of the United States. The festival, orga...

item.title: The Almazan and Fernandez families work together in the search for hidden treasure in The Secrets of Hotel 88
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios released a feature story about the continuing developments in the television series “The Secrets of Hotel 88.” The article focuses on the cooperation between the Almazan and Fernandez ...

item.title: Expansion of Pharmacy First scheme to allow pharmacists to prescribe additional medications
item.description: From this autumn, pharmacies in England will be authorised to prescribe a wider range of medications under the Pharmacy First scheme, an initiative launched in 2024 to improve access to care and reduc...

item.title: Presidency Raises Alarm Over Deepfake Videos and Religious Disinformation Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Nigerian Presidency has issued a strong warning to citizens about the rising spread of fake digital content, especially deepfake videos and manipulated audio clips being circulated online. Accordi...

item.title: Canada's Diplomatic Tone on Israel-Palestine Faces Criticism for Imbalance
item.description: Avi Benlolo critiques Prime Minister Mark Carney’s recent diplomatic engagements with Israeli President Isaac Herzog and Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas, highlighting what he sees as a t...

item.title: Opinion on Kamloops burial claims and the state of Canada’s reconciliation discourse
item.description: This opinion article by Terry Glavin examines the public and political fallout surrounding the 2021 reports of alleged unmarked graves at former residential school sites in Canada, with a particular f...

item.title: Stormers confirm key players fit ahead of URC quarter-final against Cardiff
item.description: The Stormers have finalised their team for the United Rugby Championship (URC) quarter-final against Cardiff, with captain Ruhan Nel returning at outside centre alongside Dan du Plessis. This pairing ...

item.title: Culture as the Storefront: How Authenticity is Reshaping Social Commerce in Nigeria
item.description: The article explores how modern digital commerce is increasingly driven by authenticity and cultural connection rather than traditional advertising. It describes the rise of “shoppertainment,” where s...
